title: Botrypus lunaria
intro: Botrypus lunaria, also known as Lunaria or Moonwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Ophioglossaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Botrypus lunaria include saponins, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional Chinese medicine and Western herbalism.
taxonomy: Botrypus lunaria is a species of fern within the family Osmundaceae. It is a type of ostrich fern native to North America. The classification of this plant places it in the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Osmunda, family Osmundaceae, genus Botrypus, and species B. lunaria. This classification reflects the fern's unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships to other plants. The specific classification of B. lunaria is an important aspect of understanding its biology and ecology.

preparations: Botrypus lunaria, also known as the lunar-crested fern or moonwort, has been used in herbal preparations for various purposes. Traditionally, it has been used as a treatment for respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma.
